detail: All files under output/, state/, decisions/, contracts/, history/ written by coordinator-inline (authorized writer under inline mode per file_schemas.md). State files (sections/*.json, dag.json, build-complete.json, dispatch-log.jsonl, inline-deviations/*.json) all carry coordinator-inline ownership. Output builder dirs and integration files trace back to coordinator-inline via inline dispatch. audit/ being written now by Critic.

Grepped output/integration/ for OOS keywords (login|signup|auth|tsunami|forecast|predict|subscribe|alert|export|csv|i18n|translate|bookmark) and persistence APIs (localStorage|sessionStorage|IndexedDB|cookie|download|FileReader). Only matches: role=alert (ARIA accessibility on error banner, not subscription/notification), 'Browser-global export' comments (JS module export language, not data export), Leaflet vendored code (excluded false-positives), and a comment 'No credentials/cookies; the feed is public.' (explicit disclaimer). No actual OOS feature surfaces. Per-section out_of_scope items also confirmed absent.

ledger-v1.json has version, restatement, assumption_ledger[], inflection_points[], out_of_scope[]. sections-v1.json has prompt_verb_analysis, prompt_named_verb_assertion, inflection_resolutions[], discovery_coverage_assertions[], sections[] with charter/acceptance/acceptance_assertions/depends_on/out_of_scope. Each contract has version, from/to_section, interface, notes. dag.json has nodes/edges/waves. build-complete.json has signaled_at, all_sections_verified, integrator_verified, edge_case_testing_executed, ready_for_final_verification, next_steps_required. manifest.json has version, integrated_at, entry_point, files[], runtime_network_dependencies, no_backend, verification, load_order. inline-deviations have id, ts, category, summary, context, decision, nested_equivalent, affects_product_code, affects_contract. edge-case-testing report has harness, artifact_under_test, structural_integration_checks, assertion_results, summary.

Every DAG section node maps to a state file in state/sections/ with status=verified. section-1 -> data-fetcher.js + metadata.json. section-2 -> map.js + vendor/leaflet/* + metadata. section-3 -> index.html + main.js + styles.css + metadata. section-4 -> report.json + metadata. Integrator pseudo-node verified via output/integration/manifest.json. All present.

Walked locked IP assertions against integrated artifact. constant_value (IP2.A1, TDIP2.A1, S1.A1): USGS_FEED_URL in data-fetcher.js line 14 = 'https://earthquake.usgs.gov/earthquakes/feed/v1.0/summary/all_day.geojson' — exact match. structural (S1.A2, S1.A3, TDIP2.A2, TDIP3.A2, S2.A4, S2.A6, TDIP1.A1): normalizeFeature returns the right shape, magnitudeColor returns 5 distinct hex values (#ffffb2,#fed976,#fd8d3c,#f03b20,#bd0026), index.html has and
state/escalations/sev0-fixes/ does not exist; no Sev 0 trivial fixes were logged for this run. Likely-empty case predicted in briefing matches actual.

Every prose acceptance phrase per section has at least one acceptance_assertions[] entry: section-1 acceptance phrases (loads USGS past-day feed, normalized event records, id/magnitude/place/time/lat/lon/depth fields, structured error indicator, single named constant) -> S1.A1-A5. section-2 acceptance phrases (visible interactive world map, one circle marker per event, monotonic radius, 3+ color buckets, popup with time/mag/place/depth, vendored Leaflet) -> S2.A1-A6. section-3 (renders header, dominant map region, legend with 3+ buckets, error banner with human-readable message, desktop sizing) -> S3.A1-A5. section-4 (real browser execution, coverage of all required ids, no runtime stubs) -> S4.A1-A3. Each assertion's from_acceptance_phrase quotes the source phrase.

Coverage map for ledger fields and IPs is complete: restatement -> DC.R1+DC.R2; A1-A12 -> DC.A1-A12; IP1-IP3 topic+default_branch -> DC.IP1-3 plus IP*.A*; out_of_scope[0..11] -> DC.OOS1-12; chosen_verb -> PV1; each inflection_resolutions[].chosen_option -> IP*.A* and TDIP*.A*. HOWEVER, several Coverage-Required Fields per file_schemas.md have ZERO assertions whose covers field points back to them: (a) sections[].charter — no assertion has covers='S1.charter' or 'S2.charter' etc. (charters are honored in spirit by the per-section acceptance assertions, but no covers pointer is explicit). (b) sections[].out_of_scope[] items — per-section OOS arrays have no covers entries pointing to them (project-level out_of_scope IS covered via DC.OOS*). (c) contracts/original/*.json interface methods/fields and notes — no assertion has covers pointing to a contract interface entry (e.g., 'contract.section-1--section-2.interface.fetchEarthquakes' or '.notes'). The contract shape IS implicitly enforced by S1.A2/A3/A4 and S2.A2/A3/A4 etc., but the covers field does not name the contract. Per check 8 instructions, fields with zero coverage are severity high.

dev-001 (integrator output dir output/integration/ vs contract sketch output/final/): affects_product_code=false, affects_contract=false. nested_equivalent articulates that under nested mode the Coordinator briefing's directory boundary would carry to the Integrator role identically; output/final/ is a delivery-time location, output/integration/ is the build-time location, Orchestrator copies. Plausible and not hand-wavy. dev-002 (flat layout vs js/css/ subdirs in contract sketch): affects_product_code=false, affects_contract=false. nested_equivalent articulates that the same minor layout choice would be made in nested mode for a 4-file artifact. Plausible. Neither deviation triggers changes_artifact, changes_contract, or changes_assumption — therefore no Sev 0 record required, no contract micro-adjustment to validate.

sections-v2.json declares version=2, supersedes_version=1, based_on=sections-v1.json. All v1 ids preserved (PV1, IP1-3, TDIP1-3 + their A* assertions, DC.R1-2, DC.A1-12, DC.IP1-3, DC.OOS1-12, S1.A1-5, S2.A1-6, S3.A1-5, S4.A1-3). Two new collections appended: section_coverage_assertions[] (20 entries: SC.S{1..4}.charter + SC.S{1..4}.OOS{0..3}) and contract_coverage_assertions[] (18 entries: CC.s1-s2.* x10, CC.s1-s3.* x2, CC.s2-s3.* x4, CC.int-s4.* x2). Complete superset confirmed.

impact-analysis-v2.json conforms to schema: version=2, created_at, trigger.{type,escalation_id,research_findings}, chosen_option, summary, delta_plan with per-section action+rationale (all 'unaffected' since spec-only amendment), contract_amendments=[] (no contract changes), remediation_detail with new_assertion_count=38 and new_assertion_breakdown (section_charter_coverage:4 + section_out_of_scope_coverage:16 + contract_interface_coverage:14 + contract_notes_coverage:4 = 38), covered_uncovered_fields enumerating each previously-uncovered field with its new SC/CC id, write_targets_produced, next_step.

sections-v2.json under decisions/technical-discovery/ — TD (impact mode) authorized writer per file_schemas.md write-ownership table (line 97-98). impact-analysis-v2.json under decisions/technical-discovery/ — TD (impact mode) authorized writer (one per re-evaluation). Both written by TD impact-mode dispatch, no writer-role violation.

Re-walk against sections-v2.json. (a) sections[].charter (4/4 covered): SC.S1.charter -> sections-v1.json#sections[id=section-1].charter; SC.S2.charter -> ...section-2.charter; SC.S3.charter -> ...section-3.charter; SC.S4.charter -> ...section-4.charter. (b) sections[].out_of_scope[] (16/16 covered): SC.S1.OOS0..3 -> section-1.out_of_scope[0..3]; SC.S2.OOS0..3 -> section-2.out_of_scope[0..3]; SC.S3.OOS0..3 -> section-3.out_of_scope[0..3]; SC.S4.OOS0..3 -> section-4.out_of_scope[0..3]. (c) contracts/original/*.json interface + notes (18/18 covered): CC.s1-s2.fetchEarthquakes, CC.s1-s2.EarthquakeEvent, CC.s1-s2.EarthquakeEvent.{id,magnitude,place,timeMs,lat,lon,depth}, CC.s1-s2.notes (10) + CC.s1-s3.fetchEarthquakes, CC.s1-s3.notes (2) + CC.s2-s3.{mount,magnitudeColor,magnitudeRadius,notes} (4) + CC.int-s4.{integratedArtifact,notes} (2). (d) Previously-passing coverage retained: PV1->chosen_verb; IP*.A*->IP*.chosen_option; TDIP*.A*->TDIP*.chosen_option; DC.R1-2->ledger.restatement; DC.A1-12->A1-A12; DC.IP1-3->IP1-3; DC.OOS1-12->ledger.out_of_scope[0..11]; S{1..4}.A*->section.acceptance.*. (e) All 38 new assertions carry covers fields; pointer formats match the file#json-pointer convention specified in file_schemas.md line 30. (f) All pointers resolve to fields present in the named source files. Total covered fields: 4 charters + 16 per-section OOS + 18 contract surface = 38 newly covered, plus all previously-covered (49 ledger/IP/section.acceptance items). 100% prose coverage. Sev 2b gap closed.

No regression on previously-passing schema_conformance for canonical files. ledger-v1.json, sections-v1.json (preserved as immutable history), contracts/original/*.json, dag.json, build-complete.json, manifest.json, inline-deviations/*.json, edge-case-testing report.json all retain their v1 shape. New files (sections-v2.json, impact-analysis-v2.json) conform to file_schemas.md TD-output schemas.

No regression. Re-confirmed: integrated artifact in output/integration/ contains no OOS feature surfaces (no auth, no persistence, no filters/window controls, no export/i18n/forecast/tsunami overlays, no mobile shell). Per-section OOS items (now explicitly covered by SC.S*.OOS* assertions) remain absent in the build.

No regression. Every DAG section node still maps to a verified state file: section-1 (data-fetcher.js), section-2 (map.js + vendor/leaflet/), section-3 (index.html + main.js + styles.css), section-4 (edge-case-testing report). Integrator pseudo-node verified via output/integration/manifest.json.
No regression. state/escalations/sev0-fixes/ remains absent; no Sev 0 trivial fixes were created during the impact-mode amendment (purely additive spec change, no code touched).

No regression. dev-001 and dev-002 retain affects_product_code=false / affects_contract=false; impact-mode amendment introduced no new inline deviations (TD impact mode is a deliberate spec amendment, not an inline pivot).

No regression. All locked IP/TDIP/section assertions still pass against the unchanged integrated artifact (the impact-mode amendment did not modify product code; delta_plan marks all four sections as 'unaffected'). Constant_value, structural, presence, absence, and behavior checks from prior cycle remain valid.

No regression. Every prose acceptance phrase per section retains at least one acceptance_assertions[] entry with a covers pointer (S1.A1-5, S2.A1-6, S3.A1-5, S4.A1-3 = 19 acceptance assertions, all with covers). v2 preserved these unchanged from v1.
